description: A schematic map illustrating the spatial characteristics of "INTERTIDAL FLATS" under "SEA INFLUENCE". The map shows a large blue main water body on the left, with a prominent white arrow pointing upstream, explicitly labelled "SEA INFLUENCE". Adjacent to this main blue water body is a narrow yellow strip, representing a coastal feature such as a beach or barrier island. To the right, a large, irregularly shaped grey area is depicted as "INTERTIDAL FLATS", and this area is intricately dissected by numerous lighter blue, branching channels, representing tidal creeks or waterways. The area surrounding the intertidal flats and main water body is coloured green, representing general land cover. The visual highlights the definition of intertidal flats by their network of tidal channels and the influence of the sea.
